expectations.text: problems.md explicitly names 'utils.py' as a dumping-ground / generic filename problem
expectations.evidence: Section 1 titled 'Dumping-ground filename (AR003)' directly calls out utils.py as being on the banlist.

expectations.text: problems.md calls out the generic class name 'Manager' as a naming problem
expectations.evidence: Section 2 lists 'class Manager — banlist class name' explicitly.

expectations.text: problems.md identifies __getattr__ / dynamic dispatch as a problem for AI agents
expectations.evidence: Section 3 explicitly flags DynamicAPI.__getattr__ as making the class have no discoverable surface.

expectations.text: problems.md flags the use of eval() as an anti-pattern
expectations.evidence: Section 3 flags 'eval(expr) — arbitrary code the agent cannot trace by grep or AST'.

expectations.text: problems.md identifies the deep inheritance chain
expectations.evidence: Section 5 explicitly names 'User -> Auditable -> Persisted -> Entity -> Base' as 4 levels deep.

expectations.text: problems.md notes that public functions lack type annotations / return types
expectations.evidence: Section 4 enumerates every untyped public function with 'no param type, no return type'.

expectations.text: refactored.py's public functions have type annotations on parameters and return values
expectations.evidence: evaluate_arithmetic(expression: str) -> float and sum_integers(left: int, right: int) -> int are fully annotated.

expectations.text: refactored.py does not use __getattr__ for runtime method dispatch
expectations.evidence: No __getattr__ appears in refactored.py; DynamicAPI was removed entirely.

expectations.text: refactored.py does not call eval() or exec() on user input
expectations.evidence: evaluate_arithmetic uses ast.parse with an explicit operator dispatch table; no eval or exec call exists.

expectations.text: refactored.py replaces the generic 'Manager' class name with something domain-specific (or removes it)
expectations.evidence: Manager.do_stuff was deleted and no Manager class remains in the refactored file.

expectations.text: refactored.py's inheritance chain is at most 2 levels deep (or uses composition)
expectations.evidence: AuditedUser is a flat @dataclass; UnsafeExpressionError inherits once from ValueError — both chains are shallow.

expectations.text: rationale.md grounds recommendations in specific AI-agent failure modes rather than generic clean-code arguments
expectations.evidence: Every section has a 'Failure mode prevented' subsection citing concrete agent behaviors (fabricated super calls, invisible grep, static code graph defeat).

expectations.text: refactored.py declares __all__ to make the public surface explicit (v1.3 guidance — Python-specific)
expectations.evidence: Lines 26-31 declare __all__ with AuditedUser, evaluate_arithmetic, sum_integers, UnsafeExpressionError.

expectations.text: refactored.py uses @dataclass(slots=True) or @dataclass(frozen=True) to prevent ad-hoc attribute fabrication (v1.3 guidance)
expectations.evidence: AuditedUser is declared @dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True).

expectations.text: rationale.md or problems.md explains why slots/__all__ matters for agents (hallucinated attributes, import of internal helpers) — the v1.3 framing
expectations.evidence: Rationale section 6 explains slots=True prevents silent attribute addition and section 8 explains __all__ prevents importing internal helpers.

expectations.text: layout.txt organizes by feature (auth/, billing/, users/) rather than primarily by horizontal layer
expectations.evidence: Layout has a top-level src/features/ with auth, users, billing, health subfolders each containing their own routes/handlers/queries/types.

expectations.text: layout.txt colocates test files next to source files rather than in a distant tests/ tree
expectations.evidence: Each feature folder includes *.test.ts files alongside source (e.g., auth.passwords.test.ts next to auth.passwords.ts).

expectations.text: layout.txt contains concrete domain directories like auth, billing, or users
expectations.evidence: features/auth/, features/users/, features/billing/, features/health/ are all present.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md explicitly addresses naming — avoiding generic names or requiring domain-specific names
expectations.evidence: Section 2 bans 'Manager', 'Service', 'Helper', 'Util', 'data', 'info', 'temp' and requires verbs describing full effect.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md gives a concrete file-size guideline with a number
expectations.evidence: Section 3 states 'Hard ceiling: 400 lines per file. Warn at 300.' with line length 200 chars.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md requires type annotations on public / module-boundary functions
expectations.evidence: Section 5: 'Public function signatures are fully typed. No implicit any, no unannotated returns on exported functions.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md mentions tests/lint/typecheck as the verification loop
expectations.evidence: Section 9: 'bun run check runs bun lint && bun typecheck && bun test. This is the agent's feedback loop.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md discourages dynamic/metaprogramming patterns for agent-traceability
expectations.evidence: Section 4: 'No eval, no Proxy, no runtime monkey-patching, no dynamic import()... Control flow must be visible to grep.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md explains at least one decision in terms of AI-agent behavior
expectations.evidence: Preamble states 'Optimized for maintenance by AI agents' and many rules cite agent behavior (apply-model merges, grep, etc.).

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md recommends branded / nominal types for IDs (e.g. UserId as branded string) — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 5: 'Branded IDs. UserId, SessionId, CustomerId, SubscriptionId are string & { readonly __brand: ... }'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md prefers discriminated unions (or equivalent) over magic-string dispatch / string-keyed registries — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 4/5: 'No magic-string dispatch' and 'Discriminated unions over strings' with explicit StripeEvent example.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md addresses dependency pinning, version pinning, or 'boring stack' / training-data hygiene — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 4: 'Pinned versions. package.json has no ^ or ~...' and 'Boring stack only... all in training data.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md covers deterministic tests (seeded randomness, frozen time) OR injectable side effects (clock, fetch, env as arguments) — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 6: injectable clock/logger/env/fetch via AppContext; Section 9: 'Tests freeze the clock and seed randomness.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md explicitly discourages barrel / re-export index files — v1.3 guidance (AR011)
expectations.evidence: Section 3: 'No barrel files (index.ts that only re-exports). Consumers import from the defining file.'

expectations.text: recommendations.md discusses naming with explicit reference to grep-ability, retrieval, or localization
expectations.evidence: Rec. 3 'Name things the way an agent would grep for them' explicitly ties naming to grep-based localization and model comprehension accuracy.

expectations.text: recommendations.md recommends organizing code by feature / vertical slice rather than horizontal layer
expectations.evidence: Rec. 4 'Colocate features; don't scatter across layers' explicitly contrasts vertical feature slices against horizontal layers with a concrete directory example.

expectations.text: recommendations.md warns against metaprogramming, reflection, or dynamic dispatch
expectations.evidence: Rec. 8 'Prefer static, grep-traceable code over metaprogramming' warns against __getattr__, eval, Proxy, Reflect, magic-string dispatch, and decorator stacks.

expectations.text: recommendations.md recommends typed public boundaries / type annotations at module seams
expectations.evidence: Rec. 9 'Use types as anti-hallucination anchors at boundaries' explicitly requires typed public signatures on every exported function.

expectations.text: recommendations.md recommends verification affordances tied to preventing agent hallucination spirals
expectations.evidence: Rec. 1 'Give the agent a single verification command' explicitly cites hallucination spirals and the 693-lines/39-turns failure mode.

expectations.text: recommendations.md addresses file size, near-duplicates, or str_replace breakage
expectations.evidence: Rec. 6 covers 800-line apply-model limits and 'lost in the middle'; Rec. 7 explicitly names str_replace failure on non-unique surrounding lines.

expectations.text: recommendations.md grounds reasoning in specific agent failure modes rather than generic clean-code platitudes
expectations.evidence: Names concrete failure modes including hallucination spirals, str_replace non-uniqueness, lost-in-the-middle mid-context bias, grep misses, and context-window limits.

expectations.text: recommendations.md presents the list in a prioritized order
expectations.evidence: Document is organized in explicit Tier 1/2/3 sections with a numbered ordering and an explicit 'Priority summary' top-5.

expectations.text: recommendations.md covers deterministic tests (seeded randomness, frozen time) OR injectable side effects — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Rec. 2 'Eliminate flakiness and inject side effects' covers seeded randomness, frozen time via injected clock, and injected fetch/logger/env/db.

expectations.text: recommendations.md mentions branded/nominal types OR discriminated unions over magic strings — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Rec. 9 introduces branded UserId/OrgId types; Rec. 8 shows a concrete swap from magic-string event bus to discriminated union.

expectations.text: recommendations.md flags barrel re-export files or 'export *' as an anti-pattern — v1.3 guidance (AR011)
expectations.evidence: Rec. 8 flags 'Barrel re-exports (export * from ./foo) — adds a grep hop, breaks tree-shaking'; Rec. 14 repeats the avoidance.

expectations.text: recommendations.md mentions dependency pinning OR training-data / 'boring stack' considerations — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Rec. 12 'Choose boring, well-trained frameworks' discusses training-data resemblance and explicitly recommends pinning dependency versions.

expectations.text: problems.md explicitly names 'utils.py' as a dumping-ground / generic filename problem
expectations.evidence: Section 1 'Meaningless module name (utils.py)' and the intro call it a 'dumping-ground' module.

expectations.evidence: Section 2 names process/handle/do_stuff but never mentions the class name 'Manager' specifically.

expectations.text: problems.md identifies __getattr__ / dynamic dispatch as a problem for AI agents
expectations.evidence: Section 7 '__getattr__-based dispatch (DynamicAPI)' explicitly addresses this pattern.

expectations.text: problems.md flags the use of eval() as an anti-pattern
expectations.evidence: Section 5 flags eval(expr) on caller-supplied input as a security hole and unbounded behavior surface.

expectations.text: problems.md identifies the deep inheritance chain
expectations.evidence: Section 8 names 'Base -> Entity -> Persisted -> Auditable -> User' as a 5-level inheritance chain.

expectations.text: problems.md notes that public functions lack type annotations / return types
expectations.evidence: Section 3 'No type annotations on public functions' calls this out explicitly.

expectations.evidence: identity(value: T) -> T and add_integers(left: int, right: int) -> int are fully annotated, as are mark_persisted/touch.

expectations.text: refactored.py does not use __getattr__ for runtime method dispatch
expectations.evidence: No __getattr__ defined in refactored.py; DynamicAPI was removed.

expectations.text: refactored.py does not call eval() or exec() on user input
expectations.evidence: run_expression and eval were removed entirely; no eval/exec calls remain.

expectations.text: refactored.py replaces the generic 'Manager' class name with something domain-specific (or removes it)
expectations.evidence: No Manager class exists in the refactored file.

expectations.text: refactored.py's inheritance chain is at most 2 levels deep (or uses composition)
expectations.evidence: User is a flat @dataclass with no inheritance chain.

expectations.text: rationale.md grounds recommendations in specific AI-agent failure modes rather than generic clean-code arguments
expectations.evidence: Every section has a 'Failure mode avoided' subsection naming concrete agent behaviors (fabricated types, silent no-op calls, rename refactors breaking dynamic imports).

expectations.text: refactored.py declares __all__ to make the public surface explicit (v1.3 guidance — Python-specific)
expectations.evidence: Lines 24-28 declare __all__ with User, add_integers, identity.

expectations.text: refactored.py uses @dataclass(slots=True) or @dataclass(frozen=True) to prevent ad-hoc attribute fabrication (v1.3 guidance)
expectations.evidence: User is declared as a plain @dataclass without slots=True or frozen=True.

expectations.text: rationale.md or problems.md explains why slots/__all__ matters for agents (hallucinated attributes, import of internal helpers) — the v1.3 framing
expectations.evidence: Rationale mentions __all__ preventing internal helper imports but never discusses slots/frozen semantics as a guard against hallucinated attributes.

expectations.text: layout.txt organizes by feature (auth/, billing/, users/) rather than primarily by horizontal layer
expectations.evidence: src/features/ contains auth, users, billing, health with per-feature routes/service/repo/schemas.

expectations.text: layout.txt colocates test files next to source files rather than in a distant tests/ tree
expectations.evidence: Each feature folder includes a *.test.ts (auth.test.ts, users.test.ts, billing.test.ts) colocated with source.

expectations.text: layout.txt contains concrete domain directories like auth, billing, or users
expectations.evidence: features/auth/, features/users/, features/billing/, features/health/ are all listed.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md explicitly addresses naming — doesn't allow generic names or requires domain-specific names
expectations.evidence: Dedicated 'Directory and file naming' and 'Symbol naming' sections prescribe kebab-case, <feature>.<role>.ts, and verb-noun handlers.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md gives a concrete file-size guideline with a number
expectations.evidence: File size section: 'Hard cap: 300 lines per file. Soft target: ~150 lines.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md requires type annotations on public / module-boundary functions
expectations.evidence: No explicit rule requiring type annotations on public/exported function signatures; only mentions JSDoc on public services and inferred Zod types.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md mentions tests/lint/typecheck as the verification loop
expectations.evidence: Testing section covers bun test but there is no mention of lint or typecheck as a combined verification command or loop.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md discourages dynamic/metaprogramming patterns for agent-traceability
expectations.evidence: No rules against eval, Proxy, dynamic import, monkey-patching, or magic-string dispatch; only dependency direction rules.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md explains at least one decision in terms of AI-agent behavior
expectations.evidence: Opening states 'built to be maintained primarily by AI agents' and multiple rules cite agent behavior (e.g., 'Barrels hide dependencies from agents').

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md recommends branded / nominal types for IDs (e.g. UserId as branded string) — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: No mention of branded, nominal, or opaque ID types; lib/id.ts is only 'ULID/UUID generation'.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md prefers discriminated unions (or equivalent) over magic-string dispatch / string-keyed registries — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: No guidance on discriminated unions or against magic-string dispatch/registries anywhere in the file.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md addresses dependency pinning, version pinning, or 'boring stack' / training-data hygiene — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Dependency rules: 'Pin exact versions in package.json (no ^ or ~). Upgrades are explicit commits.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md covers deterministic tests (seeded randomness, frozen time) OR injectable side effects (clock, fetch, env as arguments) — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Services take db as a parameter, lib/time.ts is described as 'Clock abstraction for tests', and tests use transactions/truncation for isolation.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md explicitly discourages barrel / re-export index files — v1.3 guidance (AR011)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly states 'Do not create index.ts barrels inside a feature. Import the exact file. Barrels hide dependencies from agents.'

expectations.text: recommendations.md discusses naming with explicit reference to grep-ability, retrieval, or localization
expectations.evidence: Rec. 1 'Make the repo navigable by grep and filename' explicitly ties naming and filenames to Grep/Glob retrieval behavior.

expectations.text: recommendations.md recommends organizing code by feature / vertical slice rather than horizontal layer
expectations.evidence: The document discusses colocation of schema/types/code and cohesive units but never explicitly recommends vertical feature slices over horizontal layers.

expectations.text: recommendations.md warns against metaprogramming, reflection, or dynamic dispatch
expectations.evidence: Rec. 20 'Avoid metaprogramming that defeats static analysis' warns against proxies, eval, dynamic this, and auto-registration.

expectations.text: recommendations.md recommends typed public boundaries / type annotations at module seams
expectations.evidence: Rec. 2 and Rec. 3 require typed self-sufficient signatures, branded IDs, and validated typed boundaries via z.infer.

expectations.text: recommendations.md recommends verification affordances tied to preventing agent hallucination spirals
expectations.evidence: Rec. 18 'Observability hooks that the agent can use to verify its work' ties fast feedback loops to preventing 'I think this works' self-deception.

expectations.text: recommendations.md addresses file size, near-duplicates, or str_replace breakage
expectations.evidence: Rec. 4 addresses file size (~200-400, above 600 guessing); does not explicitly name str_replace but discusses partial-context edits.

expectations.text: recommendations.md grounds reasoning in specific agent failure modes rather than generic clean-code platitudes
expectations.evidence: Names concrete failure modes including grep ambiguity, partial-context edits above ~600 lines, hidden side effects, and codegen overwrites, though it omits str_replace and mid-context bias specifically.

expectations.text: recommendations.md presents the list in a prioritized order
expectations.evidence: Organized in explicit Tier 1-4 sections with numbered items and a final checklist.

expectations.text: recommendations.md covers deterministic tests (seeded randomness, frozen time) OR injectable side effects — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Rec. 5 recommends passing I/O clients (db, stripe) as explicit arguments rather than module-level singletons, satisfying injectable side effects.

expectations.text: recommendations.md mentions branded/nominal types OR discriminated unions over magic strings — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Rec. 3 explicitly recommends branded/nominal IDs (UserId, OrgId, Cents) and discriminated union results.

expectations.text: recommendations.md flags barrel re-export files or 'export *' as an anti-pattern — v1.3 guidance (AR011)
expectations.evidence: Rec. 1 explicitly says 'Avoid index.ts barrel files that re-export everything' and includes it in the final checklist.

expectations.text: recommendations.md mentions dependency pinning OR training-data / 'boring stack' considerations — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: The document explicitly down-weights popular frameworks ('Use popular frameworks the AI knows ... weighted far less') and never mentions dependency pinning or training-data considerations as guidance to follow.
